user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can move a workspace to Done so completed work is grouped at the front of the workspace board workflow.
expected_behavior: The default workspace status registry includes completed with the Done label and conductor-done visuals, normalization and persistence migrations preserve the default workflow order, and workspace-status grouping renders assigned worktrees under the matching status section.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can move a workspace to In review so review-ready work is grouped separately from active implementation work.
expected_behavior: The default workspace status registry includes in-review with conductor-review visuals, worktree creation and update payloads can carry that status, drag/drop can batch-move worktrees into it, and workspace-status grouping renders the In review section.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can keep a workspace In progress by default so active work appears in the implementation lane until I move it.
expected_behavior: The default workspace status registry includes in-progress with conductor-progress visuals, the shared default workspace status id points at in-progress, normalization migrates older visuals only when requested, and workspace grouping can render customized in-progress labels.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can move a workspace to Todo so queued or not-yet-started work remains visible on the workspace board.
expected_behavior: The default workspace status registry includes todo with neutral circle visuals, normalization preserves it in the default workflow, manual ordering and workspace grouping can use workspace-status:todo group keys, and the board can create or move worktrees against status lanes.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can keep status metadata visible on workspace cards so the board still shows each workspace's current state.
expected_behavior: The worktree card property registry treats status as a fixed property, normalization restores it during hydration or direct UI writes, and the card status slot derives visible status state from workspace metadata.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can keep unread state visible on workspace cards so workspaces with new activity remain scannable.
expected_behavior: The worktree card property registry treats unread as a fixed property, normalization restores it during hydration or direct UI writes, and card/list rendering receives normalized card properties before workspace rows are displayed.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show linked GitHub issue metadata on workspace cards so issue-backed work is visible without opening details.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes issue, detailed WorktreeCard rendering shows linked issue metadata only when that property is enabled, and disabled card properties hide the issue surface.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show linked Linear issue metadata on workspace cards so Linear-backed work is visible without opening details.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes linear-issue, detailed WorktreeCard rendering shows linked Linear metadata only when that property is enabled, and persistence adds split-out default card properties for older profiles.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show linked pull request metadata on workspace cards so review state is visible from the board.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes pr, WorktreeCard rendering shows linked PR metadata when enabled, and hosted-review status icons stay hidden when the closed card properties do not permit them.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show workspace notes metadata on cards so reviewer handoff context is discoverable from the board.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes comment, detailed WorktreeCard rendering exposes a notes affordance only when comment metadata is enabled, and disabled card properties hide the notes surface.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show live port metadata on workspace cards so local servers are visible directly on the board.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes ports, WorktreeCard renders live port triggers/details only when ports are enabled, and persistence adds ports for older default card-property payloads without overriding deliberate opt-outs.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show inline agent activity on workspace cards so active agent work is visible without opening the workspace.
expected_behavior: The default worktree card property registry includes inline-agents, WorktreeCard gates the inline agent list on that property, toggling preserves fixed card properties, and persistence migrates inline agents on once while preserving later opt-outs.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open a workspace in VS Code from Open In menus so I can continue editing in my external editor.
expected_behavior: The default Open In application registry seeds VS Code with the code command, normalization trims and bounds persisted Open In targets, General settings exposes editable Open In applications, and workspace/file Open In menus use configured applications while blocking remote OS launches.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can leave terminal ligatures on Auto so ligature-capable fonts render programming ligatures without forcing them for every font.
expected_behavior: Terminal settings expose the Auto mode, defaults seed terminalLigatures to auto, the resolver enables it only for known ligature fonts, and both settings preview and live terminal appearance consume the resolved state.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can turn terminal ligatures on so the terminal attempts to render programming ligatures regardless of the selected font.
expected_behavior: Terminal settings expose the On mode, updateSettings persists terminalLigatures as on, the resolver treats on as an explicit enable, and both settings preview and live terminal appearance consume the resolved state.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can turn terminal ligatures off so the terminal keeps plain glyph rendering even when my selected font supports ligatures.
expected_behavior: Terminal settings expose the Off mode, updateSettings persists terminalLigatures as off, the resolver treats off as an explicit disable, and both settings preview and live terminal appearance consume the resolved state.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can order sidebar project groups manually so my project list stays arranged the way I choose.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Manual project ordering, persistence defaults missing or invalid projectOrderBy values to manual, upgraded profiles get the manual-order notice when project grouping is active, and project grouping can keep manual tab order independent of activity.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can order sidebar project groups by recent workspace activity so active projects rise to the top automatically.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Recent project ordering, persistence preserves explicit recent projectOrderBy values, the manual-default notice stays hidden for explicit recent ordering, and project grouping can order project headers by recent workspace activity.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can use detailed workspace cards in the sidebar so each workspace shows richer metadata without needing hover details.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Detailed card layout, the menu maps it to compactWorktreeCards false, default settings keep detailed cards enabled, and WorktreeCard renders the detailed metadata row, status/unread placement, issue, Linear, PR, notes, and repo identity surfaces when compact cards are disabled.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can use compact workspace cards in the sidebar so more workspaces fit while key details remain available from the title row and hover surface.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Compact card layout, the menu maps it to compactWorktreeCards true, persistence migrates the legacy experimental compact setting, and WorktreeCard moves unread/main indicators into the title row while compact hover details expose PR and live port context.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show compact inline agent activity on workspace cards so multiple agents summarize into a tighter sidebar affordance.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Compact agent activity display, invalid persisted values normalize back to compact, absent preferences render compact mode, and WorktreeCardAgents summarizes multiple agents behind a compact expansion affordance.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show full inline agent activity on workspace cards so each agent appears as a visible row with its own state.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Full list agent activity display, the UI slice persists agentActivityDisplayMode as full, and WorktreeCardAgents renders ordinary full rows with the Agents group label and per-agent rows instead of compact disclosure controls.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can show workspaces without project, PR, or status grouping so the sidebar stays in one general workspace list.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose None grouping, persistence preserves legacy none grouping and normalizes interim flat grouping back to none, and row building emits Pinned plus All sections with every unpinned workspace under All.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can group workspaces by status so in-progress, review, done, and custom status lanes are visible in the sidebar.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Status grouping, persistence preserves explicit workspace-status grouping, group-key generation returns workspace-status keys only in status mode, and row building emits matching status headers for assigned workspaces.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can group workspaces by PR state so review and merge status is easier to scan.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose PR grouping, row building uses PR status group keys, keeps pinned workspaces in regular PR groups, and omits empty pinned sections while preserving the PR group ordering contract.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can group workspaces by project so related worktrees, folder workspaces, and project groups stay together.
expected_behavior: The sidebar workspace options expose Project grouping, default UI preserves repo grouping, row building emits project headers, keeps empty project groups visible, supports manual or recent project ordering, and keeps imported worktrees inside project grouping.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Mobile Emulator settings so mobile emulator support can be configured for Janus Code and coding agents.
expected_behavior: The Mobile Emulator section is macOS desktop-only and renders MobileEmulatorSettingsPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Floating Workspace settings so global terminal, browser, and markdown tabs can be configured.
expected_behavior: The Floating Workspace section renders FloatingWorkspacePane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Appearance settings so theme, zoom, app/terminal appearance, sidebars, and status bar can be adjusted.
expected_behavior: The Appearance section renders AppearancePane with app font suggestions, terminal font suggestions, theme state, Ghostty, and Warp theme data.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Input & Editing settings so selection and editing behavior can be configured.
expected_behavior: The Input section renders InputPane with current settings and update callbacks.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Notifications settings so native desktop notifications for agent activity and terminal events can be configured.
expected_behavior: The Notifications section is desktop-only and renders NotificationsPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Shortcuts settings so keyboard shortcuts for common actions can be reviewed and remapped.
expected_behavior: The Shortcuts section renders ShortcutsPane when mounted and uses focused layout classes when active with no search query.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Stats & Usage settings so Janus Code plus Claude, Codex, and OpenCode usage analytics are visible.
expected_behavior: The Stats section renders StatsPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Remote Janus Code Servers settings so remote runtimes can be paired for persistent sessions and handoff.
expected_behavior: The Servers section renders RuntimeEnvironmentsPane, changes its description for web clients, and controls whether pairing URLs and local runtime are allowed.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open SSH Hosts settings so existing machines can be used for files, terminals, Git, and workspaces.
expected_behavior: The SSH section is desktop-only and renders SshPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Mobile settings so phone control for terminals and agents can be configured.
expected_behavior: The Mobile section is desktop-only, marked Beta, and renders MobileSettingsPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open macOS Permissions settings so privacy access for terminal-launched developer tools can be managed.
expected_behavior: The developer-permissions section is macOS desktop-only and renders DeveloperPermissionsPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Privacy & Telemetry settings so anonymous usage data and telemetry controls can be reviewed.
expected_behavior: The Privacy section renders PrivacyPane when mounted and receives current settings.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Advanced settings so low-level compatibility settings for troubleshooting are available.
expected_behavior: The Advanced section is desktop-only and renders AdvancedPane when mounted.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Experimental settings so preview features stay separated from stable settings.
expected_behavior: The Experimental section renders ExperimentalPane when mounted.
